condition: Shoulder injury suspected to be a rotator cuff tear or ligament damage
pain_location: Front and outer part of the shoulder, sometimes spreading down the arm
pain_nature: Sharp during movement with a deep throbbing ache at rest, accompanied by weakness and tingling sensations
duration: Persistent for the past two weeks without relief
trigger_event: Lifting a heavy object two weeks ago
previous_history: Mild strain due to physical activity in the past, no significant previous shoulder injuries
functional_limitations: Difficult to lift my arm, raise it above shoulder level, or move it normally
medical_history: No major medical conditions, overall health has been normal
red_flags: Large painful swelling (around 8–9 inches), spreading sensation, numbness with tingling in the arm
treatments_taken: Anti-inflammatory medications, rest, consultation with a doctor
